title: River gorge team separation after missed check-in
route_context: Same-day traverse with one narrow gorge segment and intermittent communication.

timeline.label: T-180
timeline.summary: All members were near the gorge entrance and the latest status was fresh.

timeline.label: T-120
timeline.summary: One member's position stopped updating while another member continued along the gorge segment.

timeline.label: T-60
timeline.summary: Replay identified possible separation from diverging freshness and checkpoint states.

timeline.label: T-30
timeline.summary: Local artifact references were enough to raise earlier awareness, but no executable skill run is part of this replay.

timeline.label: T-0
timeline.summary: Incident package marked possible separation when the team failed to regroup at the gorge exit.

post_incident_evidence.summary: Merged tracks showed one member waited near a side drainage while the other continued to the exit checkpoint.

baseline_summary: Baseline incident handling treated the missed regroup as the first strong signal.
replay_summary: Phase 2 replay surfaced possible separation from stale remote status and diverging checkpoint state before T-0.
known_outcome_summary: The team regrouped later with external help from companions; the replay does not assert a certain rescue result.

assessment.rationale: Replay provided earlier awareness of possible separation, but did not create a concrete decision window from the available facts.
